figures.caption: Fig. 1: We propose a hierarchical framework EgoMotion for Ego-VL Motion Generation, aiming to synthesize motion sequences conditioned jointly on egocentric visual observations and natural language instructions.
TABLE 1: Quantitative results of egocentric vision-language (Ego-VL) motion generation. By default, EgoMotion is equipped with a Latent Diffusion Motion Generaton with two-stage training.

Fig. 3: Qualitative comparison of motion generation across different paradigms. Generated motion sequences are visualized from a first-person perspective, conditioned on the input egocentric frame and corresponding language instructions.

TABLE 2: Ablation study on key design components of EgoMotion framework. MG denotes the motion generator, and ME refers to the motion encoder responsible for mapping motion sequences into continuous and discrete latent spaces. The symbol “-” indicates that the model performs direct prediction in the raw joint coordinate space without latent encoding.

Fig. 2: Overview of EgoMotion. The framework employs a hierarchical two-stage paradigm. (Stage I) Cognitive Reasoning Pre-training: A Gemma-2 based VLM aligns multimodal inputs (I0, L,X0) with RVQ-VAE motion primitives. (Stage II): Motion Generation: Pre-trained VLM features (HVL) condition the synthesis across three optional paradigms (Autoregressive, Masked Modeling, or Diffusion).
